Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 65/3/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 29 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:100

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper contains 29 questions divided into four sections A, B, C and D. Section A comprises of 4 questions of one mark each, Section B comprises of 8 questions of two marks each, Section C comprises of 11 questions of four marks each and Section D comprises of 6 questions of six marks each.
  3. All questions in Section A are to be answered in one word, one sentence or as per the exact requirement of the question.
  4. There is no overall choice. However, internal choice has been provided in 1 question of Section A, 3 questions of Section B, 3 questions of Section C and 3 questions of Section D. You have to attempt only one of the alternatives in all such questions.
  5. Use of calculators is not permitted. You may ask for logarithmic tables, if required.

SECTION A

Questions number 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each.

1.

Find the direction cosines of the line joining the points P(4, 3, -5) and Q(-2, 1, -8).

OR

Find the value of p for which the following lines are perpendicular: {tex}\frac{1-x}{3}=\frac{2 y-14}{2 p}=\frac{z-3}{2} ; {/tex} {tex}\frac{1-x}{3 p}=\frac{y-5}{1}=\frac{6-z}{5}{/tex}
2.

Find the integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}-2 y=2 x^2{/tex}.
3.

If A is a square matrix of order 3 with |A| = 4, then write the value of |-2A|.
4.

If y = sin-1 x + cos-1 x, find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}

SECTION B

Questions number 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each.

5.

If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 9 & 0 \\ 1 & 8 & -2 \\ 7 & 5 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{B}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 4 & 0 & 2 \\ 7 & 1 & 4 \\ 2 & 2 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find the matrix B'A'.
6.

Find: {tex}\int\limits_a^b \frac{\log x}{x} d x{/tex}
7.

Form the differential equation representing the family of curves y2 = m(a2 - x2) by eliminating the arbitrary constants m and a.
8.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\sin (x-a)}{\sin (x+a)} d x{/tex}

OR

Find: {tex}\int(\log x)^{2} d x{/tex}
9.

Find a unit vector perpendicular to both the vectors {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}, where {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}-7 \hat{j}+7 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}

OR

Show that the vectors {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}},-2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-4 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} are coplanar.
10.

Mother, father and son line up at random for a family photo. If A and B are two events given by A = Son on one end, B = Father in the middle, find P(B/A).
11.

Let X be a random variable which assumes values x1, x2, x3, x4 such that 2P(X = x1) = 3P(X = x2) = P(X = x3) = 5P(X = x4). Find the probability distribution of X.

OR

A coin is tossed 5 times. Find the probability of getting

  1. at least 4 heads, and
  2. at most 4 heads
12.

If * is defined on the set R of all real numbers by * : a * b = {tex}\sqrt{a^2+b^2}{/tex}, find the identity element, if it exists in R with respect to *.

SECTION C

Questions number 13 to 23 carry 4 marks each.

13.

Find the value of x, if {tex}\tan \left[\sec ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)\right]{/tex} = sin (tan-1 2), x > 0.
14.

If ey(x + 1) = 1, show that {tex}\frac { d ^ { 2 } y } { d x ^ { 2 } } = \left( \frac { d y } { d x } \right) ^ { 2 }{/tex}

OR

Find {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex}, if y = {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left[\frac{2^{\mathrm{x}+1}}{1+4^{\mathrm{x}}}\right]{/tex}.
15.

Find the intervals in which the function f given by f(x) = 4x3 - 6x2 - 72x + 30 is (a) strictly increasing, (b) strictly decreasing.
16.

Show that the relation R in the set Z of integers given by R = {(a, b) : 2 divides a-b} is an equivalence relation.

OR

If f(x) {tex}=\frac{4 x+3}{6 x-4}, x \neq \frac{2}{3}{/tex}, show that fof(x) = x for all {tex}x \neq \frac{2}{3}{/tex}. Also, find the inverse of f.
17.

Using properties of determinants, prove that {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \mathrm{b}+\mathrm{c} & \mathrm{a} & \mathrm{a} \\ \mathrm{b} & \mathrm{c}+\mathrm{a} & \mathrm{b} \\ \mathrm{c} & \mathrm{c} & \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 4abc
18.

If y = (sec-1 x)2, x > 0, show that {tex}x^{2}\left(x^{2}-1\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+\left(2 x^{3}-x\right) \frac{d y}{d x}-2=0{/tex}
19.

Prove that {tex}\int\limits_a^b f(x) d x=\int\limits_a^b f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} and hence evaluate {tex}\int\limits_{\frac {\pi}6}^{\frac{\pi}3} \frac{d x}{1+\sqrt{\tan x}}{/tex}.
20.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\sin 2 x}{\left(\sin ^{2} x+1\right)\left(\sin ^{2} x+3\right)} d x{/tex}
21.

Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the following lines are perpendicular to each other:
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{x}-5}{5 \lambda+2}=\frac{2-\mathrm{y}}{5}=\frac{1-\mathrm{z}}{-1};{/tex} {tex}\frac{x}{1}=\frac{y+\frac{1}{2}}{2 \lambda}=\frac{z-1}{3}{/tex}
Hence, find whether the lines intersect or not.
22.

Let {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} be three vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}|=1,|\vec{b}|=2{/tex} and {tex}|\vec{c}|=3{/tex}. If the projection of {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} along {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} is equal to the projection of {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} along {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}; and {tex}\vec{b}, \vec{c}{/tex} are perpendicular to each other, then find {tex}|3 \vec{a}-2 \vec{b}+2 \vec{c}|{/tex}.
23.

Solve the differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}

OR
23.

Solve the differential equation: (1 + x2) dy + 2xy dx = cot x dx

SECTION D

Questions number 24 to 29 carry 6 marks each.

24.

Find the area of the region {(x, y) : 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} y {tex}\leq{/tex} x2, 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} y {tex}\leq{/tex} x + 2, -1 {tex}\leq{/tex} x {tex}\leq{/tex} 3}.

OR

Evaluate {tex}\int\limits_1^4\left(1+x+e^{2 x}\right) d x{/tex} as limit of sums.
25.

Find the mean and variance of the random variable X which denotes the number of doublets in four throws of a pair of dice.
26.

Find the vector and cartesian equations of the plane passing through the points (2, 5, -3), (-2, -3, 5) and (5, 3, -3). Also, find the point of intersection of this plane with the line passing through points (3, 1, 5) and (-1, -3, -1).

OR

Find the equation of the plane passing through the intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-\hat{k})+4{/tex} = 0 and parallel to x-axis. Hence, find the distance of the plane from x-axis.
27.

Show that the height of a cylinder, which is open at the top, having a given surface area and greatest volume, is equal to the radius of its base.
28.

Hence, solve the following system of equations: x + y + z = 6, y + 3z = 11 and x - 2y + z = 0

OR

Find the inverse of the following matrix, using elementary transformations:
{tex}A=\left[ \begin{array}{lll}{2} & {3} & {1} \\ {2} & {4} & {1} \\ {3} & {7} & {2}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
29.

A company manufactures two types of novelty souvenirs made of plywood. Souvenirs of type A require 5 minutes each for cutting and 10 minutes each for assembling. Souvenirs of type B require 8 minutes each for cutting and 8 minutes each for assembling. There are 3 hours and 20 minutes available for cutting and 4 hours available for assembling. The profit is ₹ 50 each for type A and ₹ 60 each for type B souvenirs. How many souvenirs of each type should the company manufacture in order to maximize profit? Formulate the above LPP and solve it graphically and also find the maximum profit.

answer_key_html:
Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 65/3/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 29 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:100

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper contains 29 questions divided into four sections A, B, C and D. Section A comprises of 4 questions of one mark each, Section B comprises of 8 questions of two marks each, Section C comprises of 11 questions of four marks each and Section D comprises of 6 questions of six marks each.
  3. All questions in Section A are to be answered in one word, one sentence or as per the exact requirement of the question.
  4. There is no overall choice. However, internal choice has been provided in 1 question of Section A, 3 questions of Section B, 3 questions of Section C and 3 questions of Section D. You have to attempt only one of the alternatives in all such questions.
  5. Use of calculators is not permitted. You may ask for logarithmic tables, if required.

SECTION A

Questions number 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each.

1.

Find the direction cosines of the line joining the points P(4, 3, -5) and Q(-2, 1, -8).

Solution:

DRs are (6, 2, 3) {tex}\therefore{/tex} DC’s are {tex}\left(\frac{6}{7}, \frac{2}{7}, \frac{3}{7}\right){/tex}

 1

OR

Find the value of p for which the following lines are perpendicular: {tex}\frac{1-x}{3}=\frac{2 y-14}{2 p}=\frac{z-3}{2} ; {/tex} {tex}\frac{1-x}{3 p}=\frac{y-5}{1}=\frac{6-z}{5}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\frac{x-1}{-3}=\frac{y-7}{p}=\frac{z-3}{2} ; \frac{x-1}{-3 p}=\frac{y-5}{1}=\frac{z-6}{-5}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9p + p - 10 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = 1

 1
2.

Find the integrating factor of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}-2 y=2 x^2{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}-\frac{2}{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \mathrm{y}{/tex} = 2x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I.F. = e-2log x = {tex}\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex}

 1
3.

If A is a square matrix of order 3 with |A| = 4, then write the value of |-2A|.

Solution:

Given, Order of the square matrix is, n = 3
|A| = 4
|-2A| = (-2)n|A|   ...[For a square matrix of order n and constant k], {tex}|k A|=k^{n}|A|{/tex}]
|-2A| = (-2)3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
|-2A| = -32
Therefore, the value of |-2A| is -32.

 1
4.

If y = sin-1 x + cos-1 x, find {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}

Solution:

y = sin-1 x + cos-1 x
Differentiating both sides
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{d}{dx}{/tex} (sin-1 x + cos-1 x)
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{-1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} =0
OR
y = sin-1 x + cos-1 x = {tex}\frac{\mathrm\pi}2{/tex} 
Differentiating both sides
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = 0

 1

SECTION B

Questions number 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each.

5.

If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 9 & 0 \\ 1 & 8 & -2 \\ 7 & 5 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{B}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 4 & 0 & 2 \\ 7 & 1 & 4 \\ 2 & 2 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find the matrix B'A'.

Solution:

{tex}\mathrm{B}^{\prime} \mathrm{A}^{\prime}=\left[\begin{array}{lll}4 & 7 & 2 \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 4 & 6\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}3 & 1 & 7 \\ 9 & 8 & 5 \\ 0 & -2 & 4\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}75 & 56 & 71 \\ 9 & 4 & 13 \\ 42 & 22 & 58\end{array}\right]{/tex}

 2
6.

Find: {tex}\int\limits_a^b \frac{\log x}{x} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int\limits_a^b \frac{\log x}{x} d x=\left[\frac{1}{2}(\log x)^2\right]_a^b{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[(\log b)^2-(\log a)^2\right]{/tex}

 2
7.

Form the differential equation representing the family of curves y2 = m(a2 - x2) by eliminating the arbitrary constants m and a.

Solution:

The given equation is {tex}y^{2}=m\left(a^{2}-x^{2}\right),{/tex} where m and a are arbitrary constants.
{tex}y^{2}=m\left(a^{2}-x^{2}\right){/tex}........(i)
Differentiate (i) w.r.t. x,
{tex}2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=-2 m x{/tex} ..........(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow-2 m=2 \frac{y}{x} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
Differentiate (ii) w.r.t. x,
{tex}2\left[y \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}\right]=-2 m{/tex} ........(iii)
From (ii) and (iii), we get
{tex}2\left[y \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}\right]=2 \frac{y}{x} \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}
{tex}y \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}=0{/tex}
Therefore, the required differential equation is {tex}y \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}-\left(\frac{y}{x}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 0

 2
8.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\sin (x-a)}{\sin (x+a)} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int \frac{\sin (x-a)}{\sin (x+a)} d x=\int \frac{\sin (x+a-2 a)}{\sin (x+a)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int\left[\frac{\sin (x+a) \cos 2 a}{\sin (x+a)}-\frac{\cos (x+a) \sin 2 a}{\sin (x+a)}\right] d x{/tex}   [applying formula of sin(A - B)]
{tex}=\cos 2 a \int d x-\sin 2 a \int \cot (x+a) d x{/tex}
{tex}=x \cdot \cos 2 a-\sin 2 a \cdot \log |\sin (x+a)|+C{/tex}

 2

OR

Find: {tex}\int(\log x)^{2} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int(\log x)^{2} \cdot \operatorname{1dx}=(\log x)^{2} \cdot x-\int 2 \cdot \log x \cdot \frac{1}{x} \cdot x d x{/tex}
{tex}=(\log x)^{2} \cdot x-2\int \log x d x{/tex}
{tex}=x \cdot(\log x)^{2}-\left\{\log x \cdot 2 x-\int \frac{1}{x} \cdot 2 x d x\right\}{/tex}

23.

Solve the differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}

Solution:

The given differential equation is:
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex} ...(i)
Let F(x, y) = {tex}\frac{x+y}{x-y}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore F(\lambda x, \lambda y)=\frac{\lambda x+\lambda y}{\lambda x-\lambda y}=\frac{x+y}{x-y}=\lambda^{0} \cdot F(x, y){/tex}
Thus, the given differential equation is a homogeneous equation. To solve it, we make the substitution as:
y = vx
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}(y)=\frac{d}{d x}(v x){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}
Substituting the values of y and {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}in equation (i), we get
{tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x+v x}{x-v x}=\frac{1+v}{1-v}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1+v}{1-v}-v=\frac{1+v-v(1-v)}{1-v}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{1+v^{2}}{1-v}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{1-v}{1+v^{2}}\right) d v=\frac{d x}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(\frac{1}{1+v^{2}}-\frac{v}{1+v^{2}}\right) d v=\frac{d x}{x}{/tex}
Integrating both sides, we get:
{tex}\tan ^{-1} v-\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1+v^{2}\right)=\log x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \log \left[1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right]=\log x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \log \left(\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}}\right)=\log x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)-\frac{1}{2}\left[\log \left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)-\log x^{2}\right]=\log x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=\frac{1}{2} \log \left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)-logx+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
This is a required solution of the given differential equation.

Find the vector and cartesian equations of the plane passing through the points (2, 5, -3), (-2, -3, 5) and (5, 3, -3). Also, find the point of intersection of this plane with the line passing through points (3, 1, 5) and (-1, -3, -1).

Solution:

Equation of plane passing through (2, 5, -3), (-2, -3, 5) and (5, 3, -3) is
{tex}\left| \begin{array}{ccc}{x-2} & {y-5} & {z+3} \\ {-4} & {-8} & {8} \\ {3} & {-2} & {0}\end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16(x - 2) + 24(y - 5) + 32(z + 3) = 0
i.e. 2x + 3y + 4z - 7 = 0 ...(i)
which in vector form can be written as {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k})=7{/tex}
Equation of line passing through (3, 1, 5) and (-1, -3, -1) is 
{tex}\frac{x-3}{4}=\frac{y-1}{4}=\frac{z-5}{6}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{x-3}{2}=\frac{y-1}{2}=\frac{z-5}{3}{/tex} ...(ii)
Any point on (ii) is {tex}(2 \lambda+3,2 \lambda+1,3 \lambda+5){/tex}
If this is point of intersection with plane (i), then
{tex}2(2 \lambda+3)+3(2 \lambda+1)+4(3 \lambda+5)-7=0{/tex}
{tex}22 \lambda+22=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=-1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Point of intersection is (1, -1, 2)

Equation of plane through the intersection of planes

{tex}[\vec{\mathrm{r}} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}})-1]+\lambda[\vec{\mathrm{r}} \cdot(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}})+4]=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}} \cdot[(1+2 \lambda) \hat{\mathrm{i}}+(1+3 \lambda) \hat{\mathrm{j}}+(1-\lambda) \hat{\mathrm{k}}]{/tex}{tex}-1+4 \lambda=0{/tex} ...(i)

{tex}=\vec{r} \cdot(-\hat{j}+3 \hat{k})-6=0{/tex}
Distance of this plane from x–axis
{tex}=\frac{|-6|}{\sqrt{(-1)^{2}+(3)^{2}}}=\frac{6}{\sqrt{10}}{/tex}units

Show that the height of a cylinder, which is open at the top, having a given surface area and greatest volume, is equal to the radius of its base.

Solution:

Let Given surface area of open cylinder be S.
Then S = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh + {tex}\pi{/tex}r2
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{h}=\frac{\mathrm{S}-\pi \mathrm{r}^{2}}{2 \pi \mathrm{r}}{/tex}
Volume V = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
{tex}\mathrm{V}=\pi \mathrm{r}^{2}\left[\frac{\mathrm{S}-\pi \mathrm{r}^{2}}{2 \pi \mathrm{r}}\right]{/tex}{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\operatorname{Sr}-\pi r^{3}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{dr}}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\mathrm{S}-3 \pi \mathrm{r}^{2}\right]{/tex}
For maximum or minimum value, we have
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{dr}}=0{/tex}
{tex}\frac{S-3 \pi r^{2}}{2}=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad S=3 \pi r^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh + {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = r
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{dr}^{2}}=-3 \pi \mathrm{r}<0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} For volume to be maximum, height = radius

|A| = 1(7) - 1(-3) + 1(-1) = 9

or AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A-1B
